do_now.title: Pad-threshold sweep on all 14 ASAP pieces (ssl_classical_clean ep13 + released) — the calibration frontier AND the full-standing measurement in one pass
do_now.why: Highest EV-per-GPU-hour bar none. The continuous pad_prob + raw_* streams are ALREADY implemented and locally validated this session (byte-identical at 0.50, monotonic note-rescue below it). run_padsweep.sh runs eval_tuplet.py with no --pieces filter, so a single invocation simultaneously (a) sweeps the live keep-gate {0.60..0.25}, directly attacking Scriabin miss-rate (lower thr rescues dropped notes) and Mozart over-production (higher thr prunes spurious tuplets), and (b) measures all 14 test pieces, closing the '4-6 of 14 measured' gap. Zero training risk: 0.50 reproduces released behavior exactly. This produces the per-piece calibration sensitivity map that every downstream training proposal needs to target effort. Merges 4 do-now verdicts (two pad-sweep, two 14-piece-measurement) and the 'done' pad-exposure item.
do_now.est_cost: 3-6 GPU-hr (one sweep x 8 thresholds x 14 pieces; A/B the released ckpt adds same again)

do_now.title: MUSTER-based checkpoint reselection across saved epochs (ssl_classical_clean + ssl_bigc)
do_now.why: val!=MUSTER is PROVEN (ssl_bigc: best val 0.4996 but worse MUSTER via Mozart over-production 166 vs 36 tuplets). ep13 was selected by val/total and may not be the MUSTER-optimal epoch; the memory explicitly flags 'the right ckpt may be an EARLIER epoch with lower over-production' as genuinely promising and untested. Pure inference over checkpoints already on disk (save_top_k=2 + save_last). Run each saved epoch through the same 14-piece harness, add note-count-ratio and tuplet-emission-ratio as an over-production proxy, and pick the epoch minimizing 14-piece MUSTER. Free win if an earlier epoch beats ep13; at worst it confirms ep13 and yields the proxy that lets future runs checkpoint on a surrogate instead of burning full MUSTER per epoch. Merges 3 reselection verdicts. Run immediately after the threshold sweep so each epoch is scored at its own best threshold.
do_now.first_command: for c in checkpoints/ssl_classical_clean/*epoch=*.ckpt; do bash scripts/run_padsweep.sh "$c" "sslcc_$(basename $c)"; done
do_now.est_cost: 3-6 GPU-hr (a few epochs x 14 pieces; reuses sweep harness)

do_now.title: Per-head decoding sweep: greedy structural heads, mild temperature/top-k on pitch
do_now.why: Inference-only, zero retrain, orthogonal to the pad-gate and to every tested-negative data/training lever. Mechanism is sound: greedy argmax on the pitch head collapses notes in dense polyphony (Scriabin miss-rate), while pad/duration/offset must stay greedy to preserve Mozart's rhythm spine and avoid over-production. Requires a small code add (head_overrides dict threaded through generate()), defaulting to None = byte-identical current behavior, so no regression risk. Lower priority than the pad sweep because (a) it needs the small code change first, and (b) it partially overlaps the pad-gate on the same miss-rate residual — run it AFTER the pad sweep quantifies how much of Scriabin's miss-rate is keep-gate vs genuine pitch collapse, so you only spend the grid where the pad-gate left residual.
do_now.first_command: bash scripts/run_padsweep.sh 'checkpoints/ssl_classical_clean/*epoch=13*.ckpt' sslcc  # establish pad-gate baseline first, then add head_overrides to generate() and sweep --pitch-top-k/--pitch-temp

queue_high.title: Density/tuplet-rate-matched RESHAPE of the existing unpaired corpus (single canonical resampling run)
queue_high.why: ssl_bigc proved corpus COMPOSITION skew (etude-dense tail) drives Mozart over-production. This is the mechanically-opposite, untested fix: keep corpus SIZE fixed (no growth, which is what turned negative) and RESHAPE it by subsampling the ultra-dense tail / upweighting moderate pieces to match the ASAP-test density (or tuplet-rate) histogram. Density is in the manifest (n_notes/n_measures). Single retrain, hard gamma=identity control reproduces ssl_classical_clean as a sanity check. Directly attacks the exact residual that killed ssl_bigc. NOTE: the soft per-sample inverse-density WeightedRandomSampler variant (one verdict) and the explicit histogram-resampled-manifest variant (another verdict) are the SAME lever via two implementations — prefer the soft sampler version (20 lines, no manifest surgery, gamma=0 = byte-identical control). One blocker: pairs_classical_clean_manifest.csv does NOT exist locally; use the existing pairs_deduped_full/_manifest.csv (52k rows, has n_notes/n_measures) or regenerate via build_classical_clean_manifest.py first.
queue_high.est_cost: 6-10 GPU-hr per run; +6-10 per extra gamma point if sweeping

queue_high.title: Denoising-SSL: corrupt the unpaired surrogate with performance-like noise (dropout/insertion/onset-jitter), reconstruct clean score
queue_high.why: BART/MASS-style corrupt-then-reconstruct is well-established and untested here. Currently the unpaired surrogate is pure pitch-only masking (onset/duration zeroed). Adding density-scaled note dropout/insertion/jitter (onset_jitter already exists in pdmx_dataset.py) makes the model PRACTICE dense-passage recovery, attacking Scriabin miss-rate WITHOUT adding dense data (so it avoids the ssl_bigc over-production trap). Single retrain, corruption-strength is a cheap sweep if calibration is off. Distinct axis from curriculum masking (noise-robustness vs timing-info leakage).

queue_high.title: Curriculum masking of the unpaired surrogate: reveal quantized onset/duration early, anneal to pitch-only by ~60% of training
queue_high.why: Novel, not on the tested-negative list (distinct from schedule-length tests and corpus tests). Gives dense passages a recoverable early structural signal (the 1/24 grid encodes tuplet-divisible positions) before forcing pitch-only at inference. Principled curriculum-learning applied to an unaddressed lever; single retrain, bounded risk. Requires adding reveal_prob to UnpairedScoreDataset + an anneal callback. Slightly below density-reshape and denoising because the mechanism (annealing structural leakage) is more speculative than reshaping-the-known-failure-mode or standard denoising, but it directly targets the hardest residual.

queue_high.title: Auxiliary tuplet-bucket loss reweighting on the existing offset/downbeat CE
queue_high.why: Pure loss-reweighting (upweight non-dyadic/tuplet offset buckets on the 1/24 grid via F.cross_entropy weight=) — no data-distribution change, so it sidesteps the ssl_bigc failure mode entirely. All code/data claims verified; heads already exist at train and inference. Forces the shared decoder to commit to sub-beat metric resolution. Tempered to queue-high (not top) because the Scriabin tuplet tail is field-wide-hard (released SOTA also emits ~0/99), so upside is moderate — but it is a cheap, low-risk single retrain on the right residual. Run after the inference levers and the density-reshape so you know whether tuplet emission is even the binding term post-calibration.

queue_high.title: Simple->dense exposure curriculum: phase classical corpus in by density (easy->hard) across training stages
queue_high.why: Correctly diagnoses ssl_bigc as complexity-overload-from-epoch-1 and tests a NEW variable (ORDER of density exposure), not AMOUNT. Exposure order has demonstrably mattered before (ssl_unpaired vs ssl_v2 flipped Scriabin 12.68->15.15 by exposure-matching alone). Same total budget as one ssl run; phase-2 checkpoint guards against phase-3 re-triggering over-production. Kept slightly below the static density-reshape because reshape is a cleaner single-knob test of the same composition hypothesis; if reshape wins, this becomes a refinement rather than a separate slot.

queue_low.title: Difficulty-aware continuous conditioning token + density-modulated decoder dropout
queue_low.why: Reuses the existing unconditional Linear(1,d) as a continuous density input and modulates _maybe_drop_decoder by difficulty. Theoretically reasonable but speculative: beat-conditioning failed adjacently, density may be the wrong axis, and it ambitiously claims to fix BOTH residuals with one mechanism. Single retrain, but lower-confidence than the corpus-composition and denoising levers that attack the same residuals more directly. Revisit only if density-reshape shows density is the right axis.

queue_low.title: Noisy-student self-training: pseudo-label MAESTRO, fold high-confidence pairs into the paired branch (~3k:822)
queue_low.why: Sound strategy to attack the 822-pair ceiling via a different mechanism than ssl_bigc (pseudo-PAIRS not unpaired scores, with confidence gates + downweight). But blocked/unverified: MAESTRO MIDIs are not confirmed on the box (only metadata found), per-stream softmax confidence for pitch/duration is NOT currently exposed (only pad_prob is — would need code), and the referenced manifest doesn't exist. Multi-retrain (15-20 GPU-hr) with real error-amplification risk. Verify data + add confidence plumbing before greenlight; the simpler density-reshape and denoising levers are cheaper first shots at the same residuals.

queue_low.title: Confidence/difficulty-stratified pseudo-label curriculum on MAESTRO
queue_low.why: A refinement of noisy-student (stricter gates on dense windows, oversample clean dense survivors). BLOCKED by the same missing-MAESTRO-MIDI dependency and is strictly downstream of the plain noisy-student baseline working first. Speculative confidence (dense clean subset may be starved). Only pursue if MAESTRO is acquired AND plain noisy-student validates.

queue_low.title: Tuplet-rate-matched corpus resampling (explicit tuplet-rate metric variant)
queue_low.why: Same corpus-composition lever as the canonical density-reshape, but keyed on an explicit per-bucket tuplet-rate rather than density. The tuplet-bucket classification (non-dyadic indices on the 1/24 grid) is imprecisely specified in the proposal and the referenced manifest is missing. Obsoleted if density-reshape succeeds; only revisit if density-reshape fails AND tuplet-rate emerges as the binding axis. Folded under the density-reshape canonical entry.

queue_low.title: DPO/KTO preference fine-tune against a rule-based over-production/miss penalty
queue_low.why: Proven calibration technique in LLMs; rule primitives (count_tuplets, grammar rules) exist. But multi-retrain (20-40 GPU-hr), the cited paper isn't in-repo, beat-conditioning fine-tuning on this architecture was neutral-to-destructive (adding aux objectives via fine-tune is risky here), and reward-hacking one metric is a real failure mode. Explicitly should be attempted ONLY after the pad-threshold + per-head + voting calibration levers are shown insufficient, since those may close the same gap with zero/low GPU.

queue_low.title: Overlap-window voting / self-ensemble across chunk phasings
queue_low.why: Plausible boundary-note-rescue mechanism for Scriabin miss-rate, but stacks poorly with the already-implemented pad-gate (both rescue dropped notes), costs 3x compute, and needs real alignment plumbing. Strictly a follow-up: only worthwhile if the pad-threshold sweep shows a large residual of boundary-dropped notes that calibration alone can't reach.

queue_low.title: Post-hoc notational-grammar state-machine repair pass (CPU, no box)
queue_low.why: Box-free and orthogonal, but confounded by the live pad_threshold (same residuals, cleaner direct lever), leans on an unimplemented external paper, and risks music21 parse/timeout fragility that already plagues the harness. Its premise that pad-confidence isn't retained is OBSOLETE (pad_prob is now live). Only sketch defensively if measurable gaps survive optimal thresholding.

skip.title: Renderer-minted exactly-aligned synthetic pairs at <=5% of total in the paired branch
skip.reason: already_tested_negative. Synthetic-render is heavily disproven: ar_full4/5 REGRESSED MUSTER and ar_rubato DIVERGED at 7.9% — already below the proposed 5-10% threshold. The only delta is the fraction; the failure mode (synthetic-real domain gap dominates, over-production) is present even at the smaller fraction. The 822-pair ceiling is more safely attacked via density-reshape/denoising/noisy-student without synthetic domain-gap risk.

skip.title: Beat-phase-consistent offset decoding (inference-time soft Gaussian prior on offset logits)
skip.reason: already_tested_negative-adjacent. Beat-conditioning was tested and found neutral-to-destructive; the model's learned representation does not leverage beat side-info effectively, and a soft inference prior is unlikely to overcome that. Scriabin's residual is dominated by missing tuplet PRODUCTION (~0/99), not offset-bucket timing error, so this targets a secondary effect. Low EV; spend GPU on representation-level training levers instead.

skip.title: Beat/downbeat-conditioned rhythm refiner as teacher OR second-stage distillation
skip.reason: Path B (soft-target distillation) repeats training-time beat-conditioning on the same architecture that was neutral-to-destructive; Path A (ensemble merge of two unaligned models' streams) risks notational inconsistency. Both cited papers are unverifiable and the 'this differs from the failed beat test' distinction is not evidenced. Beat-alignment is ALREADY present in the ASAP chunker (tested-negative as a 'difference'). Skip unless a true zero-GPU Path A go/no-go is run and clears >0.5 Scriabin with Mozart held.

skip.title: Multi-sample self-consistency decode with note-level majority voting (fixed-slot alignment assumption)
skip.reason: Architecturally unsound as proposed: output length is set by the AR pad-stopping criterion (max_length-bounded), NOT input length, so stochastic samples have VARYING lengths and the 'trivially aligned by input-note index' premise is false. Per-slot voting would require DTW/Hungarian alignment, not the claimed 40-line aggregator. Low EV, 9-15 GPU-hr on a disproven mechanism. (The pad_prob-based voting is already subsumed by the live threshold sweep; the separate overlap-window voting idea is parked in queue_low.)

plan.top_recommendation: The moment the box is up, run ONE command — bash scripts/run_padsweep.sh 'checkpoints/ssl_classical_clean/*epoch=13*.ckpt' sslcc — then A/B the released ckpt. This single sweep is the highest-EV action in the entire backlog because it is already-implemented, zero-retrain, and does three jobs at once: it sweeps the now-LIVE keep-gate against BOTH real residuals (lower thr rescues Scriabin's dropped notes, higher thr prunes Mozart's over-produced tuplets), it measures all 14 ASAP test pieces (closing the '4-6 measured' gap and revealing whether Scriabin is truly the worst residual), and it produces the per-piece calibration frontier that tells every queued training run where to aim. Nothing should be retrained until this map exists.
plan.sequencing_notes: Order is dictated by EV-per-GPU-hour and by the fact that the inference levers produce the measurement map all training runs need. (1) The instant the box is up, run the pad-threshold sweep on ssl_classical_clean ep13 (do_now #1), then immediately A/B the released ckpt with the same script — this is the prerequisite for everything and is box-free of training risk. (2) While those run, reselect checkpoints (do_now #2): score the other saved ssl_classical_clean/ssl_bigc epochs through the same harness and pick the 14-piece-MUSTER-optimal one at its own best threshold — a potential free win and it validates the over-production proxy for future surrogate checkpointing. (3) Add the small head_overrides code and run the per-head decoding sweep (do_now #3) only on the residual the pad-gate couldn't reach, so you don't burn the grid redundantly. ONLY THEN spend GPU on training. First retrain = density/tuplet-rate corpus RESHAPE (queue_high #1, soft-sampler implementation with gamma=0 control), because it is the cleanest single-knob test of the proven ssl_bigc over-production failure mode. In parallel/next, denoising-SSL and curriculum-masking (queue_high #2,#3) attack Scriabin miss-rate on a different axis than corpus composition, so they de-risk independently. Aux-tuplet-loss and simple->dense curriculum (queue_high #4,#5) follow. Defer all MAESTRO pseudo-labeling and DPO until (a) MAESTRO MIDIs and per-stream confidence plumbing are verified to exist, and (b) the cheap calibration levers are shown insufficient — they currently are blocked or strictly dominated. Two real blockers to resolve early: pairs_classical_clean_manifest.csv does NOT exist locally (use pairs_deduped_full/_manifest.csv, which has n_notes/n_measures over 52k rows, or regenerate via build_classical_clean_manifest.py), and the GPU box may be down (every retrain item is needs_box=true; the box-free post-hoc grammar-repair pass is the only thing that can proceed offline, but it is low-priority and confounded by the live pad-gate).

verdicts.title: Curriculum masking of unpaired surrogate: reveal quantized onset early, anneal to pitch-only
verdicts.restated: Train the unpaired SSL branch with a curriculum that reveals real quantized onset/duration information early (linearly annealed to 100% masking by 60% of training), giving dense passages a recoverable early signal before forcing the final model to run on pitch-only at inference.

claims.evidence: pdmx_dataset.py:248-250 shows input_stream['onset']=zeros, input_stream['duration']=zeros, input_stream['velocity']=full(64)

claims.claim: config.FEATURES defines a 1/24 step_size grid for onset/offset/downbeat that encodes tuplet-divisible positions
claims.evidence: config.py:6-7 shows onset/offset/downbeat step_size=1/24

claims.claim: The proposal requires adding self.reveal_prob attribute to UnpairedScoreDataset and conditional masking logic in _load_pair
claims.evidence: pdmx_dataset.py:243-251 UnpairedScoreDataset._load_pair is currently fixed-masking with no reveal_prob attribute or random.random() condition; not implemented

already_tested_negative.explanation: Curriculum learning on masked-SSL is not in the tested-negative list. Prior tests covered synthetic-data scaling, corpus composition (ssl_bigc), and schedule/warmup variations. This proposes a novel per-sample masking curriculum (annealing structural info) orthogonal to those axes.

verdicts.rationale: The proposal is novel (unproven), addresses the Scriabin dense-passage miss-rate residual (the hardest unsolved target), costs one training run, and is grounded in established curriculum-learning theory applied to a well-motivated structural signal (quantized onset from a 1/24 grid that encodes tuplets). The mechanism is concrete and implementable: add reveal_prob sampling in _load_pair + a callback to anneal it. Medium confidence because curriculum masking is proven effective elsewhere but the specific application to paired-vs-unpaired onset revelation is speculative—however, the risk is bounded (single retrain) and the upside is clear (Scriabin tuplet/miss-rate improvement). It sits above queue-low because it's a principled, field-standard technique applied to an unaddressed lever, and below do-now because it requires training and has not yet been validated on this model.
verdicts.first_step: Edit pdmx_dataset.py:UnpairedScoreDataset.__init__ to add self.reveal_prob=0.0 attribute; in _load_pair (line 243-251) add: r=random.random(); if r < self.reveal_prob then keep input_stream['onset']/input_stream['duration'] from the parent load (do not zero them), else zero as current. Then add a pl.Callback in train.py that calls reveal_prob annealing in on_train_batch_start()

already_tested_negative.explanation: The proposal uses auxiliary multi-task loss reweighting on existing heads with no data-distribution change. This is distinct from the tested-negative 'more dense data' (ssl_bigc), which over-produced by changing the corpus composition. The auxiliary loss approach avoids that failure mode.

verdicts.rationale: The proposal is technically sound (all code/data claims verified), avoids the tested-negative data-change failure mode, and uses standard representation-learning techniques (auxiliary multi-task, class-balanced reweighting). However, the Scriabin tuplet tail is explicitly field-wide-hard (released SOTA also emits ~0 of 99 tuplets). The auxiliary loss is a grounded but speculative attempt to force the representation to commit to metric resolution; typical upside is moderate. One training run, low risk, but uncertain payoff. Prioritize after ruling out cheaper inference-only levers (pad-threshold sweep, checkpoint selection).
verdicts.title: Auxiliary tuplet/beat-phase prediction head from existing offset stream
verdicts.restated: Add per-class loss weighting to the existing offset/downbeat CE loss, upweighting non-dyadic (tuplet) buckets to force the shared decoder representation to resolve metric sub-beat positions, improving note placement on dense passages without changing data distribution.
verdicts.first_step: Edit train.py _compute_loss to define a per-bucket weight vector that upweights non-dyadic offset indices by factor alpha=3, then pass weight=w_offset to F.cross_entropy for the offset stream; add --tuplet-aux and --tuplet-aux-alpha CLI flags."

verdicts.title: Difficulty-aware conditioning token + per-sample decoder dropout
verdicts.restated: Replace the binary unconditional conditioning token with a continuous density/tuplet-fraction scalar, and make decoder prior-token dropout inversely proportional to density (harder chunks get more visible prior), to fix Mozart over-production and Scriabin miss-rate via better decoding calibration.

claims.evidence: embedding.py:19 confirms nn.Linear(1, config.embedding_size, bias=False); a Linear with 1-dim input trivially handles continuous scalars

claims.evidence: train.py _maybe_drop_decoder lines show: rate=0.75 paired, rate=0.50 unpaired in SSL mode; applied uniformly to all T timesteps per batch row

claims.evidence: AR_BUILD_RESULTS.md & LAB_REPORT.md: 'Best validation of all (0.4996)... NET WORSE MUSTER: Mozart 5.76->7.6 (over-produces: 166 tup vs ground-truth 36)'

claims.evidence: data/pairs_deduped_full/_manifest.csv header: 'id,src_mxl,midi,mxl,chunks,cache,n_notes,n_measures,...'; verified in Bash output

already_tested_negative.explanation: Beat-conditioning was tested as a warm-start fine-tune and shown destructive (+4.4 MeanER degradation even with gold beats, neutral in best case). However, that lever adds a NEW discrete beat-phase input stream. The current proposal reuses the EXISTING unconditional Linear and modulates it continuously, a different mechanism. Not a direct duplicate, though both are conditioning-based and one failed.

verdicts.rationale: The mechanism is theoretically sound (graded conditioning improves calibration in representation learning), and the code is straightforward (~30-50 lines). However: (1) the proposal is speculative — no evidence graded-density conditioning helps THIS model (beat-conditioning failed adjacently); (2) the dropout modulation assumption (harder chunks should be more visible to decoder) is reasonable but untested; (3) density (notes/measure) may not be the right axis (tuplet-rate or polyphony might be orthogonal); (4) the claim that this fixes BOTH Mozart over-production and Scriabin miss-rate is ambitious for one mechanism; (5) it doesn't attack the field-wide-hard tuplet tail (Scriabin emits 0/99 tuplets, a capacity/tokenization issue grading alone may not crack). It's a reasonable hedge, but less certain than simpler orthogonal levers like pad-threshold sweep or checkpoint reselection, which can be deployed inference-only. The interaction between difficulty-weighted dropout and model generalization is a plausible failure mode not yet de-risked.
verdicts.first_step: Compute density = n_notes / n_measures from the existing manifest, normalize to [0,1], and add a --difficulty-cond flag to train.py that modulates the unconditional tensor in _WithConditioning and the dropout rate in _maybe_drop_decoder; run one baseline (--difficulty-cond=False, reproduces ssl_classical_clean at 5.76/12.3/20.5 as a sanity check) and one active (--difficulty-cond=True) on the classical_clean corpus.

verdicts.title: Checkpoint selection by a MUSTER-proxy instead of val/total
verdicts.restated: During validation, compute cheap over-production / calibration metrics on the held-out ASAP validation set (emitted-note-count ratio, tuplet-bucket emission ratio), log a composite score that penalizes over-production, and select checkpoints by this proxy instead of val/total loss.

claims.claim: The proposal can be executed offline on existing ssl_classical_clean and ssl_bigc checkpoints without retraining
claims.evidence: LAB_REPORT.md: both checkpoints exist on disk; existing eval scripts (scripts/robust_eval.sh, benchmark/eval_tuplet.py) can score them without new training.

already_tested_negative.explanation: Checkpoint selection based on a proxy metric is not in the tested-negative list. However, the implicit idea that 'val != MUSTER' is acknowledged in the context ('ssl_bigc best val but worst MUSTER'), and the user explicitly states in the context: 'we might checkpoint on a surrogate so we don't burn full MUSTER eval on every epoch.' The val!=MUSTER phenomenon itself is validated (not a negative), but selecting checkpoints by MUSTER is proposed here as the remedy.

verdicts.rationale: This is one of the highest-EV, lowest-cost items: it is pure inference over existing checkpoints (no training), directly operationalizes the known val!=MUSTER divergence that ssl_bigc exemplified, and the evidence is already in hand (ssl_bigc's val 0.4996 and MUSTER regress are documented). The proxy (note-count ratio, tuplet-emission ratio) is computable from existing generate() outputs which now correctly expose pad_prob and raw streams. Even if the proxy is imperfect, it costs only a few GPU-hours of evaluation (vs the 8-12h cost of retraining), and the offline pass can immediately reveal whether an earlier epoch of ssl_classical_clean beats the val-selected one on full-14-piece MUSTER — a free win if true. verdicts.rationale: The proposal is mechanically sound and targets the exact pathology identified in ssl_bigc: dense corpus composition biases the notation prior toward over-production. Unlike the hard-cut approaches (proposals 1-3 in the same lens), this soft reweighting is minimal (20 lines), preserves the full corpus, and has a guaranteed safe fallback (gamma=0 reproduces ssl_classical_clean byte-for-byte). It directly operationalizes the hypothesis 'dense sample overexposure causes Mozart over-production' and can sweep the knob to find a Pareto optimum (low Mozart over-production + best Scriabin). The gamma=0 control is critical for validation. Risk is low because downweighting dense samples should not hurt, only suppress spurious notation. However, it will NOT crack the Scriabin tuplet tail (dense exposure is likely required for that); it is purely an anti-over-production calibration lever. This is queue-high not do-now because it requires one training run and GPU box, but it is high-confidence and not disproven.

verdicts.rationale: Plausible mechanism: notes near chunk boundaries may indeed be decoded once with weak context and dropped; voting across phased decodes could recover them. Scriabin's miss-rate (~14% of dense notes dropped) is the exact target. HOWEVER: (1) This assumes boundary effects are significant—unquantified. (2) The proposal stacks poorly with the pad-threshold sweep (proposal[0], already implemented), which also rescues dropped notes. Both attack the same mechanism from different angles—voting via context diversity vs. threshold calibration. The pad-threshold sweep is simpler, cheaper (no 3× compute), and better understood (continuous on the actual keep-probability). (3) Risk of variant collapse: if Mozart is truly deterministic at boundaries (plausible but unproven), voting adds 3× compute for no gain. (4) The implemented detokenize (line 468-469) already supports voting on pad_prob directly; per-note voting on multi-phasing would require significant new plumbing (wrapper, alignment, aggregation). Recommend pairing with pad-threshold sweep to understand the baseline first (what fraction of notes are actually boundary-dropped vs. poor keep-probability). If pad-threshold exhausts the low-hanging fruit, revisit voting as a follow-up refinement."

verdicts.title: Multi-sample self-consistency decode with note-level majority voting
verdicts.restated: Run N stochastic decodes per piece (temperature > 1.0, top_k > 1) and majority-vote predictions at the note level, assuming output sequences are trivially aligned by input-note index.

claims.claim: The architecture is a per-input-note translator where 'each output note corresponds to a fixed encoder input note' and 'N samples are already aligned by input-note index'
claims.evidence: model.py:33/85: generate() loops `range(max_length + 1 - y['pad'].shape[1])` where max_length=512 (default) is independent of input length T. Output length is determined by AR stopping criterion (when the model generates padding tokens), not input length. Empirically, output length varies with stochastic samples due to different padding probabilities. No guaranteed input-output alignment mechanism exists.

claims.claim: Voting can be implemented as a trivial ~40-line aggregator that majority-votes per slot without sequence alignment
claims.evidence: If output lengths vary across N samples (confirmed by variable AR termination), per-slot voting requires first aligning sequences (DTW, Hungarian matching, or re-chunking). The proposal's claim of 'trivially aligned by construction since chunking is by note index with fixed 512/128 overlap' conflates input chunking (which is fixed) with output alignment (which is variable). The 40-line estimate is unverifiable without solving the alignment problem first.

claims.claim: The default decoding is strictly greedy: top_k=1, temperature=1.0, single pass
claims.evidence: model.py:33 default parameters, utils.py:69-70 default infer() signature, infer_with_ckpt.py:40-41 CLI defaults all confirm top_k=1 and temperature=1.0. The generate() loop (model.py:85-162) shows multinomial sampling at each step with per-stream logits, confirming single-pass greedy decoding.

claims.evidence: infer_with_ckpt.py:40-41 expose --top-k (int, default 1) and --temperature (float, default 1.0), both passed through to infer() at line 60-62.

This was documented as 'already implemented' in the task briefing.

already_tested_negative.explanation: This proposal is not a tested-negative lever; it is a novel inference-time aggregation idea. However, it is architecturally unsound due to the output-length variability issue, which is not a tested lever but a fundamental misunderstanding of the model's decoding mechanism.

verdicts.est_gpu_hours: 9-15 (N=3-5 stochastic decodes, 5 pieces, ~1.5-3 hours per piece × N)
verdicts.rationale: The core claim that output sequences are 'trivially aligned by input-note index' is architecturally unsound. The model generates sequences of variable length (determined by the AR loop's stopping criterion based on pad logits), not fixed to input length. Multiple stochastic samples will have different output lengths, making per-slot voting impossible without expensive sequence alignment (DTW or re-chunking). Even if corrected, the expected gain (0.3-1.0 MeanER on Scriabin, per the proposal) is speculative, and multinomial sampling at top_k>1 risks degrading pitch accuracy. The proposal's 40-line aggregator is a significant underestimate of the actual alignment/voting code needed. Cost-benefit is poor: 9-15 GPU-hours for an uncertain, low-EV gain on a disproven mechanism.
verdicts.first_step: Do NOT implement. If slot-alignment voting is genuinely desired, first implement a sequence-alignment step (DTW or note-matching) and quantify the alignment overhead before committing to stochastic sampling. This is beyond the scope of an 'inference-only' proposal.

already_tested_negative.which: Not a duplicate of beat-conditioning fine-tune
already_tested_negative.explanation: The tested-negative beat-conditioning experiment fine-tuned the monolithic model on beat tokens (TRAINING-time intervention). This proposal uses preference optimization on the model's own greedy outputs (INFERENCE-time reward scoring + preference fine-tune). The mechanisms are orthogonal. However, beat-conditioning being neutral suggests that adding auxiliary signal via fine-tuning this model is risky and may not transfer.

verdicts.rationale: The proposal is technically sound in principle—DPO/KTO on model-generated preference pairs is a proven calibration-fixing technique in LLMs. The rule primitives (count_tuplets, grammar rules, note-count thresholds) already exist in the codebase. However, three factors push this to queue-low: (1) The arXiv:2604.18489 paper is not available in the repo and the exact mechanism hasn't been studied in music yet, so confidence is medium rather than high. (2) Beat-conditioning fine-tuning on this model was tested and found neutral-to-destructive, suggesting that adding auxiliary objectives via fine-tuning this particular architecture is risky. (3) There is a cheaper, higher-confidence alternative: pad_threshold sweeping (already implemented, inference-only, no training) can directly quantify the over-production/miss-rate trade-off and may yield a 0.5-1.0 MeanER gain with zero GPU. DPO should be attempted AFTER confirming that pad_threshold+voting+per-head-decoding don't suffice, because once those calibration levers are exhausted, DPO becomes the next-best candidate. The 20-40 GPU hours is non-trivial and the risk of reward-hacking or overshooting one metric at the expense of another is real.
verdicts.first_step: Before attempting DPO fine-tuning, run the complete pad_threshold sweep (inference-only, 0 GPU cost) on all 14 ASAP test pieces with both ssl_classical_clean and released checkpoints, to quantify whether decoding calibration alone can close the over-production/miss-rate gap. Command: bash scripts/robust_eval.sh ssl_classical_clean benchmark/full_clean.txt <all 14 piece substrings> at pad-thresholds {0.30, 0.35, 0.40, 0.45, 0.50, 0.55}—if this shows joint improvement on both Scriabin and Mozart, DPO is unnecessary.

verdicts.rationale: The proposal is logically sound and targeting the right residuals, but has three material blockers: (1) **confounded by pad_threshold sweep** — the 'ALREADY DONE' pad_prob exposure makes pad_threshold live, providing a cleaner direct lever on the same miss/over-production failure modes; any grain from repair logic will be hard to isolate; (2) **unimplemented grounding** — the proposal leans on arXiv:2510.02746 but provides zero working algorithm, implementation details, or code; reconstructing a state machine from a paper is high-effort and high-risk for marginal gain; (3) **brittleness and music21 fragility** — modifying music21 streams before postprocess_score risks introducing parsing failures and hanging.
